GERWIG_THEMES_DB.title: Coming of Age
GERWIG_THEMES_DB.shortDesc: The messy, beautiful process of becoming yourself
GERWIG_THEMES_DB.fullDesc: From Lady Bird's senior year to Jo March's artistic awakening to Barbie's existential crisis, Gerwig returns again and again to the moment when a person — usually a young woman — realizes who she is by reckoning with who she isn't. Her coming-of-age stories are never simple; they're about the gap between the person you want to be and the person you actually are.

GERWIG_THEMES_DB.title: The Female Gaze
GERWIG_THEMES_DB.shortDesc: Reframing cinema through women's lived experience
GERWIG_THEMES_DB.fullDesc: Gerwig's films don't just tell women's stories — they see the world the way women see it. The camera lingers on details men's films skip: the way a mother and daughter can wound each other with love, the economics of being a woman artist in the 19th century, the absurdity of being a doll designed for someone else's fantasy.

GERWIG_THEMES_DB.title: Literary Adaptation
GERWIG_THEMES_DB.shortDesc: Reinventing classic texts for contemporary audiences
GERWIG_THEMES_DB.fullDesc: Gerwig's Little Women proved that literary adaptation doesn't mean reverence — it means conversation. By restructuring Alcott's novel with a non-linear timeline and a metafictional ending, she made a 150-year-old book feel urgent. Even Barbie, adapted from a toy line, became a meditation on existentialism worthy of Simone de Beauvoir.

GERWIG_THEMES_DB.title: Comedy & Pathos
GERWIG_THEMES_DB.shortDesc: Finding the funny in the painful and the painful in the funny
GERWIG_THEMES_DB.fullDesc: Gerwig's tonal signature is the ability to make you laugh and cry in the same scene. Frances Ha's pratfalls are tinged with desperation. Lady Bird's fights with her mother are simultaneously hilarious and devastating. Barbie's existential crisis plays as both farce and genuine philosophy. This isn't tonal whiplash — it's emotional truth.

GERWIG_THEMES_DB.title: Sacramento & Place
GERWIG_THEMES_DB.shortDesc: The specificity of where you're from shapes who you become
GERWIG_THEMES_DB.fullDesc: Lady Bird is one of cinema's great love letters to a specific place — not New York or LA but Sacramento, a city rarely given its cinematic due. Gerwig understands that your hometown isn't just a backdrop; it's a character in your story, one you spend your whole life either embracing or trying to escape.

GERWIG_THEMES_DB.title: Creative Collaboration
GERWIG_THEMES_DB.shortDesc: The Baumbach partnership and Gerwig's repertory company
GERWIG_THEMES_DB.fullDesc: Gerwig's creative life is defined by collaboration — with Noah Baumbach (co-writer, partner), with Saoirse Ronan (her muse across two films), with Sam Levy (her cinematographer). Like the best filmmaker-actor partnerships (Scorsese-De Niro, Coogler-Jordan), these relationships deepen over time, producing work that neither could make alone.

Saoirse Ronan.signature: Two iconic Gerwig heroines — the restless teenager and the ambitious writer
Saoirse Ronan.pullQuote: Greta sees you in a way that makes you braver than you thought you could be.

Timoth\u00e9e Chalamet.signature: From Lady Bird's brooding love interest to Little Women's lovesick neighbor

Margot Robbie.signature: Transformed a plastic doll into a genuinely moving character study

Florence Pugh.signature: Reinvented the most misunderstood March sister as the film's secret weapon

Ryan Gosling.signature: Turned Ken's identity crisis into one of 2023's most memorable performances

Laurie Metcalf.signature: Oscar-nominated turn as the mother who loves too hard and says too much

Noah Baumbach.contribution: Co-wrote Frances Ha, Mistress America, and Barbie — Gerwig's creative counterpart and intellectual sparring partner

Sam Levy.contribution: Creates the warm, naturalistic visual style that defines Gerwig's films

Jon Brion.contribution: Scored Lady Bird with an intimate, piano-driven palette — also known for Eternal Sunshine, Punch-Drunk Love, and producing Fiona Apple

Alexandre Desplat.contribution: Two-time Oscar winner who brought sweeping, period-perfect romanticism to Little Women — also scored The Grand Budapest Hotel and The Shape of Water

Mark Ronson.contribution: Grammy and Oscar-winning producer who shaped Barbie's eclectic pop soundtrack — known for 'Uptown Funk' and Amy Winehouse's Back to Black

Jacqueline Durran.contribution: Oscar-winning costume designer who brought the March sisters to life — also designed for Anna Karenina and 1917

Rodrigo Prieto.contribution: Three-time Oscar-nominated DP who shot Barbie's candy-colored dreamscapes — also known for Brokeback Mountain, The Irishman, and Killers of the Flower Moon

Sarah Flack.contribution: Frequent Sofia Coppola collaborator who edited Lady Bird — brings a delicate, rhythm-driven editing sensibility

Nick Houy.contribution: Edited both Little Women and Barbie for Gerwig — a trusted partner in shaping the rhythm and emotional arc of her films

GERWIG_FILMOGRAPHY.title: Frances Ha
GERWIG_FILMOGRAPHY.description: A love letter to New York, female friendship, and the beautiful messiness of your twenties.

GERWIG_FILMOGRAPHY.title: Mistress America
GERWIG_FILMOGRAPHY.description: A screwball comedy about ambition, betrayal, and the stories we tell about each other.

GERWIG_FILMOGRAPHY.title: Lady Bird
GERWIG_FILMOGRAPHY.description: A semi-autobiographical coming-of-age masterpiece set in Sacramento. Five Oscar nominations.

GERWIG_FILMOGRAPHY.title: Little Women
GERWIG_FILMOGRAPHY.description: A radical reimagining of Louisa May Alcott's novel with a non-linear structure and metafictional ending.

GERWIG_FILMOGRAPHY.title: Barbie
GERWIG_FILMOGRAPHY.description: The highest-grossing film of 2023. An existential comedy that turned a toy brand into feminist philosophy.
